methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $104M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Alice & Bob is a Series B quantum computing company founded by Theau Peronnin and Raphael Lescanne that has raised $104M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a competitive set of 11 quantum computing peers: 3 public companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private firms including Q-CTRL, Quantinuum, and IonQ.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable triangulation approach, given insufficient narrow-peer comparability in the quantum computing sector. Using total capital raised ($104M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical Series B-to-subsequent-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x yields an implied valuation of $198M. A peer-median approach was not pursued due to an insufficient comparable set: while 7 same-sector peers exist, filtering for identical stage, adjacent stage, and similar valuation tier yielded fewer than 3 datapoints, making sector-wide median analysis unreliable for a frontier technology company at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $168M (low) to $227M (high), with a median estimate of $198M. This range reflects conservative application of stage-progression mechanics against the $104M capital base. Key uncertainties include the absence of a robust peer-comparable set specific to Series B quantum computing businesses, and the nascent nature of the sector's revenue and profitability benchmarks. Any refined estimate would require additional late-stage or exit comparable data as the quantum computing market matures.
